name: Imam Bayildi (Braised Eggplant with Tomatoes
recipeIngredient: 6 medium Onion (peeled, sliced into rings, 1/8 inch thick)
5 medium Tomato (fresh, ripe, peeled, seeded and finely chopped, or 1 1/2 cup chopped, drained canned tomatoes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the stem and peel each eggplant lengthwise, leaving 4 evenly spaced 1-inch wide strips of peel intact. Slicing between the strips, cut each one in half. Cut side up, make three or four 4-inch long lengthwise slashes through the thickest part of each half, spacing the slashes about 1 inch apart.
2. Sprinkle the eggplants with 1 tablespoon of salt and arrange them in two or three layers in a large flat bowl or pan. Pour in enough cold water to cover them by 1 inch, weight with a heavy casserole, and let the eggplants rest at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, place onion rings in a large colander set in a deep plate. Sprinkle onions with remaining 3 tablespoons of salt, turning them about with a spoon to coat evenly. Let stand at room temperature for at least 30 minutes, then rinse the onions under warm running water and squeeze gently but completely dry.
4. Place rinsed onions in a bowl, add chopped tomatoes and remaining 2 teaspoons of salt, tossing thoroughly.
5. Pour 2 tablespoons of olive oil into a heavy casserole large enough to hold eggplants in one layer. Drain eggplants, rinse under cold water, pat dry with paper towels.
6. Arrange eggplants cut side up in the casserole. Force onion-tomato mixture into slashes and spread remaining mixture on top. Place garlic clove on each eggplant half and sprinkle with remaining 6 tablespoons of oil.
7. Pour in cup of water and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until eggplants are tender. Cool in casserole at room temperature.
8. To serve, arrange eggplants on a large platter or individual serving plates, spoon cooking juices around them and sprinkle with parsley.
